methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Nordic Air Defence is a seed-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Karl Rosander. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, comprising 2 public companies and 8 private peers including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

The peer-median method anchors the primary valuation approach. The five comparables span a wide range: Tenna Systems at $60M (seed-stage), Ares Industries at $150M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Stark Defence at $500M, and Scale AI at $29.0B. The median valuation across this set is $300M. This dispersion—from $60M to $29.0B—reflects material heterogeneity in funding stage, product maturity, and addressable market within the defense technology sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on the $300M peer median. Key uncertainties constraining precision include the stage mismatch in the comp set (most peers are Series A, while Nordic Air Defence is seed-stage, typically warranting a discount) and the extreme outlier of Scale AI's $29.0B valuation, which skews the distribution. Without additional valuation methods or proprietary financial data on Nordic Air Defence's revenue trajectory or capital efficiency, reliance on peer multiples alone provides directional guidance rather than a tight point estimate. The analyst should seek comparable transaction data and conduct sensitivity analysis around stage-appropriate discounts before finalizing any valuation opinion.
